description: GTM Optimization Tier 3 — Agent architecture: fact sheet schema, verifier agent, reply classifier, banned pattern gate, scheduled sweeps

userStories.title: Create research fact sheet schema and playbook
userStories.description: As a research sub-agent, I need a documented JSON schema so my output is structured, verifiable, and grounded — preventing hallucinated hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at gtm/playbooks/research-fact-sheet-schema.md

Contains the full JSON schema with all fields: company, contact, title, email, email_confidence, channel, icp_score, signal (type/detail/source_url/date), data_need, verified_facts array, disqualifiers array, existing_relationship object
signal.source_url is documented as REQUIRED — no URL means signal must be null
existing_relationship fields documented: in_attio, in_smartlead, in_gmail, contacts_master_match — all must be checked before research proceeds
Contains the research sub-agent prompt template from the hardened spec
Contains 2 example fact sheets: one for a demand-side company with a real signal, one with signal: null (no verifiable signal found)
Contains a section on what copywriter agents receive: ONLY this JSON, nothing else
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema from gtm/GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 3.1. This is a playbook document, not code. The examples should use real companies from the knowledge graph for realism.

userStories.title: Create verifier agent prompt and skill file
userStories.description: As the QA pipeline, I need a verifier agent that checks every email draft against its fact sheet before the adversarial scoring step.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at gtm/playbooks/verifier-agent.md

Documents the 6 checks in order: hallucination check, signal URL check, channel check (supply must not mention claru.ai), copy rules check (em-dash, opening with I/We, >80 words, >5 word subject, meeting CTA), posture check (founder not consultant), dedup check (existing_relationship all false)
Contains the verifier prompt template that takes two inputs: the fact sheet JSON and the email draft
Output format documented: { pass: boolean, failures: [...], revision_note: string }
Documents the revision loop: max 2 attempts, then human escalation
Contains 2 test examples: one email that passes all checks, one that fails on hallucination (references something not in fact sheet)
Referenced from gtm/agents.md as a new agent role in the pipeline (between copywriter and QA supervisor)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: From GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 3.2. This sits between the copywriter and the QA supervisor in the pipeline. Update gtm/agents.md to include the verifier role.

userStories.title: Create reply classification prompt and test suite
userStories.description: As the reply handling system, I need a documented classification prompt with a test suite proving 90%+ accuracy on historical replies.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at gtm/playbooks/reply-classifier.md
Contains the 7-category classification prompt: interested, question, objection, ooo, unsubscribe, referral, not_interested
Prompt extracts: return_date (for OOO), referred_contact (for referral), confidence score 0.0-1.0
Documents the confidence threshold: below 0.75 routes to human review
Documents that the classifier must NEVER generate response content — classify and route only
Contains a test suite section with at least 10 example replies and their expected classifications, sourced from gtm/contacts-master.md reply log and pipeline/PIPELINE.md
Examples cover all 7 categories: at least 1 interested, 1 question, 1 objection, 1 OOO with parseable date, 1 unsubscribe, 1 referral, 1 not_interested, plus 3 ambiguous cases
Documents how this integrates with the handle-smartlead-reply Edge Function from Tier 2
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: From GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 3.3. The Edge Function already uses a simple classification prompt — this creates the canonical reference version with test cases.

userStories.title: Create banned pattern gate script
userStories.description: As the copy quality pipeline, I need a deterministic pre-send filter that catches AI slop before the LLM-based adversarial scoring runs.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at scripts/banned-pattern-gate.py (or .ts)
Accepts an email object: { subject, body, channel } as input

Checks all hard-reject patterns: em-dash anywhere, 'I'd love to', 'I'd be happy to', 'I hope this finds you well', 'impressive work', 'exciting work', 'I came across', 'In today's landscape/market/environment', opens with 'I ' or 'We ' as first word, subject >5 words, body >80 words, multiple questions (count ? > 1), any hyperlink, banned words (delve, leverage, comprehensive, pivotal, synergy, transformative, revolutionize, seamless)
Supply-side additional checks when channel=supply: mentions 'claru.ai' or 'Claru' anywhere = hard reject
Returns JSON: { pass: boolean, violations: [string] }
Includes inline test cases: at least 5 emails that should pass and 5 that should fail with specific violations
Running the script with --test flag executes all test cases and reports pass/fail
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: From GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 4.1. This runs BEFORE the LLM adversarial scoring to save tokens on obviously bad copy. Python preferred since the outbound skill already uses Python scripts.

userStories.title: Update agents.md with complete upgraded pipeline
userStories.description: As the agent orchestrator, I need agents.md to reflect the full Tier 1-3 architecture: research fact sheets, verifier agent, reply classifier, and banned pattern gate.
acceptanceCriteria: gtm/agents.md updated with the complete pipeline: Orchestrator → Research Agents (fact sheet output) → Copywriter Agents (fact sheet input only) → Banned Pattern Gate (static) → Verifier Agent (fact sheet cross-check) → QA Supervisor (adversarial scoring) → Human Review Gate → Loader Agent
Research Agent section references gtm/playbooks/research-fact-sheet-schema.md
Verifier Agent section added between Copywriter and QA Supervisor, references gtm/playbooks/verifier-agent.md
Reply Handler section added: references handle-smartlead-reply Edge Function and gtm/playbooks/reply-classifier.md
Banned Pattern Gate section references scripts/banned-pattern-gate.py
campaign_leads table documented as shared blackboard (from Tier 2)
Existing content about prospector agents, conflict checker, and loader preserved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the keystone story that ties Tiers 1-3 together. agents.md becomes the single reference for how the entire agent sales team operates.

userStories.title: Validation — verify all Tier 3 playbooks and scripts work
userStories.description: As the GTM system owner, I need to verify all new playbooks are internally consistent and the banned pattern gate script runs correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: gtm/playbooks/research-fact-sheet-schema.md exists and JSON schema is valid
gtm/playbooks/verifier-agent.md exists with all 6 checks documented
gtm/playbooks/reply-classifier.md exists with 10+ test examples
scripts/banned-pattern-gate.py exists and runs without errors
Running banned-pattern-gate.py --test passes all test cases
gtm/agents.md includes research fact sheets, verifier, reply classifier, and banned pattern gate
gtm/index.md updated with entries for new playbook files
All cross-references between files are valid (no broken links)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: QA story. Run the banned pattern gate tests, verify all file cross-references, and spot-check playbook content against the hardened spec.
